docs.title: LimelightAdView (Android)
docs.text: LimelightAdView is a ViewGroup subclass that loads and displays a banner ad.

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
Create an instance, configure the ad unit ID and size, then add it to your view hierarchy.

val adView = LimelightAdView(context)
adView.setAdUnitId("your-ad-unit-id")
adView.setAdSizes(LimelightAdSize(320, 50))
adView.setAdViewListener(listener)
adView.loadAd()

adContainer.addView(adView)

LimelightAdView sizes itself based on the content. Use LayoutParams to position it within your layout.

docs.title: Ad Sizes
docs.text:
Constructor Dimensions Common name LimelightAdSize(320, 50) 320 × 50 dp Standard banner LimelightAdSize(300, 250) 300 × 250 dp Medium rectangle LimelightAdSize(728, 90) 728 × 90 dp Leaderboard

Use setAdSizes() to specify the primary ad size before calling loadAd().

val adView = LimelightAdView(context)
adView.setAdSizes(LimelightAdSize(320, 50))
adView.loadAd()

docs.title: Ad Format
docs.text:
By default LimelightAdView requests a banner creative. Use setAdFormats() before calling loadAd() to request different formats.

adView.setAdFormats(LimelightAdFormat.VIDEO)
adView.loadAd()

Supported formats: LimelightAdFormat.BANNER, LimelightAdFormat.VIDEO or both.

docs.title: Loading an Ad
docs.text:
Call loadAd() once the view is in the hierarchy. If auto-refresh interval is set, the SDK refreshes the ad automatically after each interval.

adView.loadAd()

docs.title: Auto-Refresh
docs.text:
The ad refreshes automatically at a configurable interval. Set the interval in seconds before calling loadAd().

adView.setAutoRefreshInterval(30)
adView.loadAd()

docs.title: Listener — Ad Lifecycle
docs.text:
Set a listener implementing LimelightAdViewListener to receive ad lifecycle events. All callbacks are invoked on the main thread.

Method When it fires onAdLoaded(adView, ad) Ad loaded and rendered successfully onAdFailedToLoad(adView, error) Load or render error; inspect the LimelightError for details onAdImpression(adView) Ad became visible to the user onAdClicked(adView) User tapped the ad onAdClosed(adView) The ad was closed 
val listener = object : LimelightAdViewListener {
    override fun onAdLoaded(adView: LimelightAdView, ad: LimelightAd) {
        Log.d(TAG, "Ad loaded")
    }

    override fun onAdFailedToLoad(adView: LimelightAdView, error: LimelightError) {
        Log.e(TAG, "Ad failed: ${error.message}")
    }

    override fun onAdImpression(adView: LimelightAdView) {
        Log.d(TAG, "Ad displayed")
    }

    override fun onAdClicked(adView: LimelightAdView) {
        Log.d(TAG, "Ad clicked")
    }

    override fun onAdClosed(adView: LimelightAdView) {
        Log.d(TAG, "Ad closed")
    }
}

docs.title: Constructor
docs.text:
LimelightAdView(context: Context)

Creates a banner ad view.

docs.title: Methods
docs.text: Method Description setAdUnitId(adUnitId: String) Sets the ad unit ID for the ad request setAdSizes(adSize: LimelightAdSize) Sets the ad size setAdFormats(vararg formats: LimelightAdFormat) Sets the creative formats to request setAutoRefreshInterval(seconds: Int) Sets the refresh interval in seconds setAdViewListener(listener: LimelightAdViewListener) Sets the listener for ad lifecycle callbacks loadAd() Starts the ad load and enables auto-refresh destroy() Releases resources; call in onDestroy()

docs.title: Complete Example
docs.text:
class BannerActivity : AppCompatActivity() {

    private var adView: LimelightAdView? = null

    override fun onCreate(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
        super.onCreate(savedInstanceState)
        setContentView(R.layout.activity_banner)

        val adContainer = findViewById<ViewGroup>(R.id.ad_container)

        // 1. Create ad view
        adView = LimelightAdView(this).apply {
            // 2. Configure ad view
            setAdUnitId("your-ad-unit-id")
            setAdSizes(LimelightAdSize(320, 50))
            setAutoRefreshInterval(30)
            setAdViewListener(object : LimelightAdViewListener {
                override fun onAdLoaded(adView: LimelightAdView, ad: LimelightAd) {
                    Log.d(TAG, "Banner ad loaded")
                }

                override fun onAdFailedToLoad(adView: LimelightAdView, error: LimelightError) {
                    Log.e(TAG, "Banner ad failed: ${error.message}")
                }

                override fun onAdImpression(adView: LimelightAdView) {
                    Log.d(TAG, "Banner ad displayed")
                }

                override fun onAdClicked(adView: LimelightAdView) {
                    Log.d(TAG, "Banner ad clicked")
                }

                override fun onAdClosed(adView: LimelightAdView) {
                    Log.d(TAG, "Banner ad closed")
                }
            })

            // 3. Load ad
            loadAd()
        }

        // 4. Add to the UI
        adContainer.addView(adView)
    }

    override fun onDestroy() {
        super.onDestroy()
        adView?.destroy()
    }
}

docs.title: Limelight Mediation Applovin Integration Guide for Android
docs.text:
This guide explains how to integrate the Limelight SDK into your Android app using AppLovin MAX mediation.

Prerequisite: This approach requires the AppLovin SDK to be installed. For more information, follow the AppLovin Android Integration Guide.

docs.title: Step 1: Add Limelight SDK Dependencies
docs.text:
First, add the Limelight Maven repository to your root-level build.gradle (or settings.gradle) file:

allprojects {
    repositories {
        maven { url 'https://limelight-sdk.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/android' }
    }
}

Next, add the Limelight Core SDK and the AppLovin Adapter to your app-level build.gradle file:

dependencies {
    implementation("com.limelight.sdk:core:1.0.0")
    implementation("com.limelight.sdk:applovin-adapters:1.0.0")
}

docs.title: Step 2: Initialize the SDK
docs.text:
The Limelight adapter supports automatic initialization as long as the App ID is provided in your AppLovin ad unit configuration.

However, we highly recommend manually initializing the SDK in your Application class. This speeds up the ad request process during application startup.

class DemoApplication : Application() {
    override fun onCreate() {
        super.onCreate()

        val endpointUrl = "https://ads-forty-flsk9l.ortb.net/inapp"
        LimelightSDK.initialize(endpointUrl, this) { status ->
            if (status.initializationFailed()) {
                Log.e("LimelightSDK", "SDK initialization error: ${status.description}")
            }
        }
    }
}

docs.title: Step 3: Set Up Limelight in the AppLovin Dashboard
docs.text:
You need to register Limelight as a Custom Network within your AppLovin dashboard.

  1. Navigate to Networks in the AppLovin dashboard.
  2. Click on Create Custom Network.
  3. Fill in the following details exactly as shown:
    • Custom Network Name: Limelight
    • iOS Adapter Class Name: Limelight
    • Android Adapter Class Name: com.limelight.sdk.applovin.LimelightAppLovinMediationAdapter

Note: For more details on this process, see AppLovin's Official guide on integrating custom networks.

docs.title: Step 4: Configure Ad Units
docs.text:
To monetize using Limelight, you must enable it for your specific AppLovin Ad Units.

  1. Navigate to Ad Units in the AppLovin dashboard.
  2. Select the Ad Unit you want to monetize with Limelight.
  3. Scroll down to Custom Networks and activate Custom Network - Limelight.
  4. Configure the following fields:
  5. App ID: The URL to your in-app endpoint (Format: test.ortb.net/inapp).
    • Placement ID: Any custom string/name you prefer.
    • Custom Parameters: A valid JSON configuration string (see details below).

docs.title: Custom Parameters (JSON)
docs.text:
The Custom Parameters field requires a JSON object to define your ad unit ID, sizes, and formats.

Example JSON:

{
  "ad_unit_id": "<YOUR_AD_UNIT_ID>",
  "sizes": [
    {
      "w": 320,
      "h": 50
    }
  ],
  "ad_formats": [
    "banner"
  ]
}

Parameter Descriptions:

  • ad_unit_id (Required): The unique ID for this ad unit, generated in the Limelight dashboard.
  • sizes (Optional): An array of objects specifying the width (w) and height (h) of the ad.
  • ad_formats (Optional): An array specifying accepted ad formats. Valid values are "banner" and "video".

docs.title: Consent Management (Android)
docs.text: The Limelight SDK reads privacy and consent signals from your Consent Management Platform (CMP) and forwards them to every bid request automatically. No additional code is required beyond ensuring your CMP writes the standard IAB SharedPreferences keys before the SDK loads ads.

docs.title: Supported Frameworks
docs.text: Framework Source Forwarded in bid request TCF 2.0 (GDPR) CMP (SharedPreferences) regs.gdpr, regs.ext.gdpr, regs.ext.consent CCPA (US Privacy) CMP (SharedPreferences) regs.ext.us_privacy GPP CMP (SharedPreferences) regs.gpp, regs.gpp_sid COPPA Programmatic regs.coppa

docs.title: TCF 2.0 / GDPR
docs.text:
The SDK reads the standard IAB TCF 2.0 SharedPreferences keys written by your CMP. No additional code is required — the SDK picks up these values automatically before each bid request.

SharedPreferences key Description IABTCF_gdprApplies Whether GDPR applies ("1" / "0") IABTCF_TCString Base64-encoded TCF consent string IABTCF_PurposeConsents Purpose-level consent bitstring

The CMP is the sole source of truth for these values. They are not settable via the SDK API.

docs.title: CCPA (US Privacy String)
docs.text:
The SDK reads the CCPA US Privacy string written by your CMP to SharedPreferences:

SharedPreferences key Description IABUSPrivacy_String IAB US Privacy string (e.g., "1YNN")

The value is read automatically and forwarded to bid requests as regs.ext.us_privacy.

docs.title: COPPA
docs.text:
COPPA is the only consent signal set programmatically. Set it before loading ads for apps directed at children.

LimelightSDK.setSubjectToCoppa(true)

When set to true, the SDK sets regs.coppa = 1 on every bid request.

docs.title: GPP (Global Privacy Platform)
docs.text:
The SDK reads the standard IAB GPP keys written by your CMP to SharedPreferences:

SharedPreferences key Description IABGPP_HDR_GppString Full GPP consent string → regs.gpp IABGPP_GppSID Applicable section IDs (underscore-separated, e.g. "2_6_7") → regs.gpp_sid (integer array)

The SDK parses the underscore-separated SID string written by the CMP into an integer array before forwarding it.

docs.title: LimelightSDK
docs.text: Property Type Description setSubjectToCoppa Boolean? Marks the request as subject to COPPA. The only consent signal configurable via the SDK API. Use Targeting.shared — not Limelight — to set this.

docs.title: Complete Example
docs.text:
class CustomApplication : Application() {

    override fun onCreate() {
        super.onCreate()
        LimelightSDK.setSubjectToCoppa(true)
        LimelightSDK.initialize(...)
    }

}

docs.title: LimelightSDK (Android)
docs.text: LimelightSDK is the main entry point of the Limelight Android SDK. It provides SDK initialization, runtime configuration, and debug controls. All setup should happen at app launch, before any ads are requested.

docs.title: Initialization
docs.text:
Call LimelightSDK.initialize() as early as possible — typically in your Application.onCreate() method.

class CustomApplication : Application() {

    override fun onCreate() {
        super.onCreate()
        LimelightSDK.initialize("https://ads-forty-flsk9l.ortb.net/openrtb/526274869", this) { status ->
            if (status.initializationFailed()) {
                Log.e(TAG, "SDK initialization error: ${status.description}")
            }
        }
    }

}

The listener parameter is optional. If you do not need to react to the initialization result, you can pass null:

LimelightSDK.initialize("https://ads-forty-flsk9l.ortb.net/openrtb/526274869", this, null)

docs.title: Custom Targeting
docs.text:
Attach custom targeting data to all ad requests via the setTargeting() method.

val targeting = LimelightTargeting()
targeting.setUserAge(25)
targeting.setKeywords(listOf("sports", "tech"))

LimelightSDK.setTargeting(targeting)

For all targeting fields setting see the InAppBannerTargeting example in the demo app.

docs.title: Log Level
docs.text:
Control the verbosity of SDK logs. By default, logging is disabled.

LimelightSDK.setLogLevel(LimelightLogLevel.DEBUG) // for debugging
LimelightSDK.setLogLevel(LimelightLogLevel.DISABLE) // production

docs.title: Geolocation Sharing
docs.text:
Enable sharing of geolocation data with the SDK (disabled by default):

LimelightSDK.setShareGeoLocation(true)

docs.title: COPPA Compliance
docs.text:
Set COPPA (Children's Online Privacy Protection Act) compliance status:

LimelightSDK.setSubjectToCoppa(true)

docs.title: SDK Version Info
docs.text: Log.d("Limelight", "SDK Version: " + LimelightSDK.getVersion())

docs.title: Complete Setup Example
docs.text:
class CustomApplication : Application() {

    override fun onCreate() {
        super.onCreate()

        // Debug (remove in production)
        LimelightSDK.setLogLevel(LimelightLogLevel.DEBUG)

        // Geolocation
        LimelightSDK.setShareGeoLocation(true)

        // Targeting
        val targeting = LimelightTargeting()
        targeting.setKeywords(Arrays.asList("news", "finance"))
        LimelightSDK.setTargeting(targeting)

        // Privacy
        LimelightSDK.setSubjectToCoppa(false)

        LimelightSDK.initialize("https://ads-forty-flsk9l.ortb.net/openrtb/526274869", this) { status ->
            if (status.initializationFailed()) {
                Log.e(TAG, "SDK initialization error: ${status.description}")
            }
        }
    }

}

docs.title: LimelightInterstitial (Android)
docs.text: LimelightInterstitial loads and displays a full-screen interstitial ad. Load the ad in advance, wait for the listener confirmation, then present it.

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
Create an instance with a Context and ad unit ID, set a listener, and call loadAd().

// 1. Create interstitial
val adUnit = LimelightInterstitial(this, "your-ad-unit-id")

// 2. Configure ad unit
adUnit.setAdSizes(LimelightAdSize(getAdWidth(), getAdHeight()))
adUnit.setInterstitialListener(createListener())

// 3. Load ad
adUnit.loadAd()

docs.title: Loading an Ad
docs.text:
Call loadAd() to begin fetching the ad. The SDK notifies you via onAdLoaded() when the ad is ready to show.

adUnit.loadAd()

docs.title: Showing an Ad
docs.text:
To show the ad immediately after the ad is loaded, call show() in the onAdLoaded callback.

override fun onAdLoaded(interstitial: LimelightInterstitial, ad: LimelightAd) {
    adUnit.show()
}

docs.title: Ad Sizes
docs.text:
By default the interstitial occupies the full screen. Use setAdSizes() to set the expected creative size.

adUnit.setAdSizes(LimelightAdSize(320, 480))
adUnit.loadAd()

docs.title: Ad Formats
docs.text:
By default the SDK requests both BANNER and VIDEO formats. Set ad formats before calling loadAd() to restrict or change which formats are requested.

adUnit.setAdFormats(LimelightAdFormat.VIDEO)
adUnit.loadAd()

Supported formats: LimelightAdFormat.BANNER, LimelightAdFormat.VIDEO.

docs.title: Listener — Ad Lifecycle
docs.text:
Set a listener implementing LimelightInterstitialListener to receive ad lifecycle events.

Method When it fires onAdLoaded(interstitial, ad) Ad loaded and ready to display onAdFailedToLoad(interstitial, error) Load error; inspect the LimelightError for details onAdImpression(interstitial) Ad became visible to the user onAdClicked(interstitial) User tapped the ad onAdClosed(interstitial) User dismissed the full-screen ad 
val listener = object : LimelightInterstitialListener {
    override fun onAdLoaded(interstitial: LimelightInterstitial, ad: LimelightAd) {
        adUnit.show()
    }

    override fun onAdFailedToLoad(interstitial: LimelightInterstitial, error: LimelightError) {
        Log.e(TAG, "Interstitial failed: ${error.message}")
    }

    override fun onAdImpression(interstitial: LimelightInterstitial) {
        // Ad impression tracked
    }

    override fun onAdClicked(interstitial: LimelightInterstitial) {
        // Ad clicked
    }

    override fun onAdClosed(interstitial: LimelightInterstitial) {
        // Ad closed
    }
}

docs.title: Constructor
docs.text:
LimelightInterstitial(context: Context, adUnitId: String)

Creates an interstitial ad unit for the given ad unit ID.

docs.title: Methods
docs.text: Method Description setInterstitialListener(listener) Sets the listener for ad lifecycle events setAdSizes(vararg sizes: LimelightAdSize) Sets creative sizes for the ad setAdFormats(vararg formats: LimelightAdFormat) Sets creative formats to request loadAd() Starts loading the ad show() Presents the ad; call only after isReady is true destroy() Releases resources; call in onDestroy()

docs.title: Complete Example
docs.text:
class InterstitialActivity : AppCompatActivity() {

    private var interstitialAd: LimelightInterstitial? = null

    override fun onCreate(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
        super.onCreate(savedInstanceState)

        // 1. Create interstitial
        interstitialAd = LimelightInterstitial(this, "your-ad-unit-id")

        // 2. Configure ad unit
        interstitialAd?.setAdSizes(LimelightAdSize(320, 480))
        interstitialAd?.setInterstitialListener(createListener())

        // 3. Load ad
        interstitialAd?.loadAd()
    }

    override fun onDestroy() {
        super.onDestroy()
        interstitialAd?.destroy()
    }

    private fun createListener(): LimelightInterstitialListener =
        object : LimelightInterstitialListener {
            override fun onAdLoaded(interstitial: LimelightInterstitial, ad: LimelightAd) {
                // 4. Show ad
                interstitialAd?.show()
            }

            override fun onAdImpression(interstitial: LimelightInterstitial) {
                // Ad impression tracked
            }

            override fun onAdFailedToLoad(interstitial: LimelightInterstitial, error: LimelightError) {
                // Handle error
            }

            override fun onAdClicked(interstitial: LimelightInterstitial) {
                // Ad clicked
            }

            override fun onAdClosed(interstitial: LimelightInterstitial) {
                // Ad closed
            }
        }
}

docs.title: LimelightRewarded (Android)
docs.text: LimelightRewarded loads and displays a full-screen rewarded ad. Load the ad in advance, wait for the listener confirmation, present it, then grant the reward when onUserEarnedReward fires.

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
Create an instance with a Context and an ad unit ID, set a listener, and call loadAd().

// 1. Create rewarded
val adUnit = LimelightRewarded(this, "your-ad-unit-id")

// 2. Configure ad unit
adUnit.setRewardedListener(createListener())
adUnit.setAdSizes(LimelightAdSize(getAdWidth(), getAdHeight()))

// 3. Load ad
adUnit.loadAd()

docs.title: Loading an Ad
docs.text:
Call loadAd() to begin fetching the ad. The SDK notifies you via onAdLoaded when the ad is ready to show.

adUnit.loadAd()

docs.title: Showing an Ad
docs.text:
To show the ad immediately after the ad is loaded, call show() in the onAdLoaded callback.

override fun onAdLoaded(adUnit: LimelightRewarded, ad: LimelightAd) {
    adUnit.show()
}

docs.title: Handling the Reward
docs.text:
Implement onUserEarnedReward to grant the in-app reward once the user has earned it. The Reward object describes the reward type and amount defined for your ad unit.

override fun onUserEarnedReward(adUnit: LimelightRewarded, reward: Reward?) {
    // Grant the reward to the user
}

Important: Always grant the reward inside onUserEarnedReward, not onAdClosed. The reward callback fires only when the user has fulfilled the watch requirement; onAdClosed fires regardless.

docs.title: Ad Sizes
docs.text:
By default the rewarded ad occupies the full screen. Use setAdSizes() to set the primary creative size.

adUnit.setAdSizes(LimelightAdSize(320, 480))
adUnit.loadAd()

docs.title: Ad Formats
docs.text:
By default the SDK requests both BANNER and VIDEO formats. Call setAdFormats() before loadAd() to restrict or change which formats are requested.

adUnit.setAdFormats(LimelightAdFormat.VIDEO)
adUnit.loadAd()

Supported formats: LimelightAdFormat.BANNER, LimelightAdFormat.VIDEO.

docs.title: Listener — Ad Lifecycle
docs.text:
Set a listener implementing LimelightRewardedListener to receive ad lifecycle events. All callbacks are invoked on the main thread. All methods are optional.

Method When it fires onAdLoaded(adUnit, ad) Ad loaded and ready to display onAdFailedToLoad(adUnit, error) Load error; inspect the LimelightError for details onAdImpression(adUnit) Ad became visible to the user onAdClicked(adUnit) User tapped the ad onUserEarnedReward(adUnit, reward) User earned the reward onAdClosed(adUnit) User dismissed the full-screen ad 
val listener = object : LimelightRewardedListener {
    override fun onAdLoaded(adUnit: LimelightRewarded, ad: LimelightAd) {
        adUnit.show()
    }

    override fun onAdFailedToLoad(adUnit: LimelightRewarded, error: LimelightError) {
        Log.e("Rewarded", "Failed to load: $error")
    }

    override fun onAdImpression(adUnit: LimelightRewarded) {}

    override fun onAdClicked(adUnit: LimelightRewarded) {}

    override fun onUserEarnedReward(adUnit: LimelightRewarded, reward: Reward?) {
        // Grant the reward to the user
    }

    override fun onAdClosed(adUnit: LimelightRewarded) {
        // Ad closed
    }
}

docs.title: Constructor
docs.text:
LimelightRewarded(context: Context, adUnitId: String)

Creates a rewarded ad unit for the given ad unit ID.

docs.title: Methods
docs.text: Method Description setRewardedListener(listener) Sets the listener for ad lifecycle callbacks setAdSizes(vararg sizes) Sets the primary creative size(s) setAdFormats(vararg formats) Sets creative formats to request loadAd() Starts loading the ad show() Presents the ad; call only after isReady is true destroy() Cleans up resources; must be called when the ad unit is no longer needed

docs.title: Complete Example
docs.text:
class RewardedActivity : AppCompatActivity() {

    private var rewardedAd: LimelightRewarded? = null

    override fun onCreate(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
        super.onCreate(savedInstanceState)

        // 1. Create rewarded ad
        rewardedAd = LimelightRewarded(this, "your-ad-unit-id")

        // 2. Configure ad unit
        rewardedAd?.setRewardedListener(createListener())
        rewardedAd?.setAdSizes(LimelightAdSize(320, 480))

        // 3. Load ad
        rewardedAd?.loadAd()
    }

    override fun onDestroy() {
        super.onDestroy()
        rewardedAd?.destroy()
    }

    private fun createListener(): LimelightRewardedListener = object : LimelightRewardedListener {
        override fun onAdLoaded(adUnit: LimelightRewarded, ad: LimelightAd) {
            // 4. Show ad
            adUnit.show()
        }

        override fun onAdFailedToLoad(adUnit: LimelightRewarded, error: LimelightError) {
            Log.e("Rewarded", "Failed to load: $error")
        }

        override fun onAdImpression(adUnit: LimelightRewarded) {}

        override fun onAdClicked(adUnit: LimelightRewarded) {}

        override fun onUserEarnedReward(adUnit: LimelightRewarded, reward: Reward?) {
            Log.d("Rewarded", "User earned reward: $reward")
            // Grant the reward to the user
        }

        override fun onAdClosed(adUnit: LimelightRewarded) {
            // Ad closed
        }
    }
}

docs.title: LimelightAdView
docs.text: LimelightAdView is a UIView subclass that loads and displays a banner ad. Add it to your view hierarchy, set a delegate, and call loadAd().

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
Create an instance with an ad unit ID and one of the standard ad sizes, then add it to your view hierarchy.

import LimelightSDK

let adView = LimelightAdView(adUnitId: "your-ad-unit-id", adSize: .banner_320x50)
adView.delegate = self
view.addSubview(adView)

LimelightAdView sizes itself to match the chosen LimelightAdSize. Use Auto Layout or manual frames to position it within your layout.

docs.title: Ad Sizes
docs.text:
Case Dimensions Common name banner_320x50 320 × 50 pt Standard banner banner_300x250 300 × 250 pt Medium rectangle banner_728x90 728 × 90 pt Leaderboard

Use additionalCreativeSizes to allow the ad slot to request and display creatives larger or smaller than the primary size. Set it before calling loadAd().

let adView = LimelightAdView(adUnitId: "your-ad-unit-id", adSize: .banner_320x50)
adView.additionalCreativeSizes = [CGSize(width: 300, height: 50)]
adView.loadAd()

docs.title: Ad Format
docs.text:
By default LimelightAdView requests a banner creative. Set adFormat before calling loadAd() to request a different format.

adView.adFormat = .video
adView.loadAd()

Supported formats: .banner or .video.

docs.title: Loading an Ad
docs.text:
Call loadAd() once the view is in the hierarchy. If autoRefreshInterval is set, the SDK refreshes the ad automatically after each interval.

adView.loadAd()

docs.title: Auto-Refresh
docs.text:
The ad refreshes automatically at a configurable interval. The valid range is 15 – 120 seconds; the default is 60 seconds.

adView.autoRefreshInterval = 30  // seconds
adView.loadAd()

To permanently stop auto-refresh (e.g. when the view scrolls off screen):

adView.stopRefresh()

Note: stopRefresh() is permanent for the current load cycle. Call loadAd() again to resume.

docs.title: Delegate — Ad Lifecycle
docs.text:
Set delegate to an object conforming to LimelightAdViewDelegate to receive ad lifecycle events. All callbacks are invoked on the main thread. All methods are optional.

Method When it fires onAdLoaded(_:) Ad loaded and rendered successfully onAdFailedToLoad(_:_:) Load or render error; inspect the LimelightError for details onAdImpression(_:) Ad became visible to the user onAdClicked(_:) User tapped the ad and is leaving the app onAdOpened(_:) A modal (e.g. landing page) is about to be presented onAdClosed(_:) The modal was dismissed 
extension MyViewController: LimelightAdViewDelegate {
    func onAdLoaded(_ adView: LimelightAdView) {
        print("Ad loaded")
    }

    func onAdFailedToLoad(_ adView: LimelightAdView, _ error: LimelightError) {
        print("Ad failed: \(error)")
    }
}

docs.title: Delegate — Video Playback
docs.text:
Set videoPlaybackDelegate to an object conforming to LimelightAdViewVideoPlaybackDelegate to receive video playback events. All callbacks are invoked on the main thread. All methods are optional.

Method When it fires onVideoPlaybackDidPause(_:) Video paused onVideoPlaybackDidResume(_:) Video resumed onVideoPlaybackWasMuted(_:) Video muted onVideoPlaybackWasUnmuted(_:) Video unmuted onVideoPlaybackDidComplete(_:) Video played to completion 
extension MyViewController: LimelightAdViewVideoPlaybackDelegate {
    func onVideoPlaybackDidComplete(_ adView: LimelightAdView) {
        print("Video finished")
    }
}

docs.title: Initializer
docs.text:
init(adUnitId: String, adSize: LimelightAdSize)

Creates a banner ad view. The view's frame is set automatically to match adSize.

docs.title: Properties
docs.text: Property Type Access Description adSize LimelightAdSize read-only The ad size specified at init time adFormat AdFormat read/write The creative format to request: .banner, .video, or .native. Defaults to .banner additionalCreativeSizes [CGSize]? read/write Extra sizes the ad slot can accept, in addition to adSize autoRefreshInterval TimeInterval read/write Refresh interval in seconds (15 – 120, default 60) delegate LimelightAdViewDelegate? read/write Receives ad lifecycle callbacks videoPlaybackDelegate LimelightAdViewVideoPlaybackDelegate? read/write Receives video playback callbacks

docs.title: Methods
docs.text: Method Description loadAd() Starts the first ad load and enables auto-refresh stopRefresh() Permanently stops auto-refresh for the current load cycle

docs.title: Complete Example
docs.text:
import UIKit
import LimelightSDK

class BannerViewController: UIViewController {

    private let adView = LimelightAdView(adUnitId: "your-ad-unit-id", adSize: .banner_320x50)

    override func viewDidLoad() {
        super.viewDidLoad()

        adView.delegate = self
        adView.autoRefreshInterval = 30
        view.addSubview(adView)

        // Centre at the bottom of the safe area
        adView.translatesAutoresizingMaskIntoConstraints = false
        NSLayoutConstraint.activate([
            adView.centerXAnchor.constraint(equalTo: view.centerXAnchor),
            adView.bottomAnchor.constraint(equalTo: view.safeAreaLayoutGuide.bottomAnchor)
        ])

        adView.loadAd()
    }
}

extension BannerViewController: LimelightAdViewDelegate {
    func onAdLoaded(_ adView: LimelightAdView) {
        print("Banner ad loaded")
    }

    func onAdFailedToLoad(_ adView: LimelightAdView, _ error: LimelightError) {
        print("Banner ad failed: \(error)")
    }
}

docs.title: Limelight Mediation Applovin Integration Guide for iOS
docs.text:
This guide explains how to integrate the Limelight SDK into your iOS app using AppLovin MAX mediation.

Prerequisite: This approach requires the AppLovin SDK to be installed. For more information, follow the AppLovin iOS Integration Guide.

docs.title: Cocoapods
docs.text:
• Add this to your Podfile:
pod "LimelightSDKAppLovinAdapter"
  • Then run:
pod install --repo-update

docs.title: SPM
docs.text:
• Add this url as an Xcode package dependency:
https://github.com/ProjectLimelight/limelight-adapter-applovin-spm.git
  • Or add to your Package.swift
dependencies: [
    .package(url: "https://github.com/ProjectLimelight/limelight-adapter-applovin-spm.git", .upToNextMajor(from: "1.0.0"))
]

docs.title: Step 2: Initialize the SDK
docs.text:
The Limelight adapter supports automatic initialization as long as the App Id is provided in your AppLovin ad unit configuration.

However, we highly recommend manually initializing the SDK in your AppDelegate class. This speeds up the ad request process during application startup.

@main
final class AppDelegate: UIResponder, UIApplicationDelegate {
    var window: UIWindow?

    func application(_ application: UIApplication, didFinishLaunchingWithOptions launchOptions: [UIApplication.LaunchOptionsKey: Any]? = nil) -> Bool {
        Limelight.initialize(host: "https://ads-forty-flsk9l.ortb.net/inapp", completion: { result in
            switch result {
            case let .success(status):
                print("Limelight successfully initialized: \(status)")
            case let .failure(error):
                print("Limelight initialization failed: \(error)")
            }
        })
        return true
    }
}

docs.title: Step 3: Set Up Limelight in the AppLovin Dashboard
docs.text:
You need to register Limelight as a Custom Network within your AppLovin dashboard.

  1. Navigate to Networks in the AppLovin dashboard.
  2. Click on Create Custom Network.
  3. Fill in the following details exactly as shown:
    • Custom Network Name: Limelight
    • iOS Adapter Class Name: Limelight
    • Android Adapter Class Name: com.limelight.sdk.applovin.LimelightAppLovinMediationAdapter

Note: For more details on this process, see AppLovin's Official guide on integrating custom networks.

docs.title: Step 4: Configure Ad Units
docs.text:
To monetize using Limelight, you must enable it for your specific AppLovin Ad Units.

  1. Navigate to Ad Units in the AppLovin dashboard.
  2. Select the Ad Unit you want to monetize with Limelight.
  3. Scroll down to Custom Networks and activate Custom Network - Limelight.
  4. Configure the following fields:
    • App ID: The URL to your in-app endpoint (Format: test.ortb.net/inapp).
    • Placement ID: Any custom string/name you prefer.
    • Custom Parameters: A valid JSON configuration string (see details below).

docs.title: Custom Parameters (JSON)
docs.text:
The Custom Parameters field requires a JSON object to define your ad unit ID, sizes, and formats.

Example JSON:

{
    "ad_unit_id": "<YOUR_AD_UNIT_ID>",
    "sizes": [
        {
            "w": 320,
            "h": 50
        }
    ],
    "ad_formats": ["banner"]
}

Parameter Descriptions:

  • ad_unit_id (Required): The unique ID for this ad unit, generated in the Limelight dashboard.
  • sizes (Optional): An array of objects specifying the width (w) and height (h) of the ad.
  • ad_formats (Optional): An array specifying accepted ad formats. Valid values are "banner" and "video".

docs.title: Consent Management
docs.text: The Limelight SDK reads privacy and consent signals from your Consent Management Platform (CMP) and forwards them to every bid request automatically. No additional code is required beyond ensuring your CMP writes the standard IAB UserDefaults keys before the SDK loads ads.

docs.title: Supported Frameworks
docs.text: Framework Source Forwarded in bid request TCF 2.0 (GDPR) CMP (UserDefaults) regs.gdpr, regs.ext.gdpr, regs.ext.consent CCPA (US Privacy) CMP (UserDefaults) regs.ext.us_privacy GPP CMP (UserDefaults) regs.gpp, regs.gpp_sid COPPA Programmatic regs.coppa

docs.title: TCF 2.0 / GDPR
docs.text:
The SDK reads the standard IAB TCF 2.0 UserDefaults keys written by your CMP. No additional code is required — the SDK picks up these values automatically before each bid request.

UserDefaults key Description IABTCF_gdprApplies Whether GDPR applies ("1" / "0") IABTCF_TCString Base64-encoded TCF consent string IABTCF_PurposeConsents Purpose-level consent bitstring

The CMP is the sole source of truth for these values. They are not settable via the SDK API.

docs.title: CCPA (US Privacy String)
docs.text:
The SDK reads the CCPA US Privacy string written by your CMP to UserDefaults:

UserDefaults key Description IABUSPrivacy_String IAB US Privacy string (e.g., "1YNN")

The value is read automatically and forwarded to bid requests as regs.ext.us_privacy.

docs.title: COPPA
docs.text:
COPPA is the only consent signal set programmatically. Set it before loading ads for apps directed at children.

Targeting.shared.subjectToCOPPA = true

When set to true, the SDK sets regs.coppa = 1 on every bid request.

docs.title: GPP (Global Privacy Platform)
docs.text:
The SDK reads the standard IAB GPP keys written by your CMP to UserDefaults:

UserDefaults key Description IABGPP_HDR_GppString Full GPP consent string → regs.gpp IABGPP_GppSID Applicable section IDs (underscore-separated, e.g. "2_6_7") → regs.gpp_sid (integer array)

The SDK parses the underscore-separated SID string written by the CMP into an integer array before forwarding it.

docs.title: Targeting.shared
docs.text: Property Type Description subjectToCOPPA Bool? Marks the request as subject to COPPA. The only consent signal configurable via the SDK API. Use Targeting.shared — not Limelight — to set this.

docs.title: Complete Example
docs.text:
import LimelightSDK

// Called after your CMP has finished and written UserDefaults values.
func onConsentReady() {

    // COPPA — set for apps directed at children
    Targeting.shared.subjectToCOPPA = false

    // Initialize the SDK. TCF, CCPA and GPP signals from UserDefaults
    // are included in every bid request automatically.
    Limelight.initialize(host: "https://your-ad-server.example.com") { result in
        switch result {
        case .success:
            break
        case .failure(let error):
            print("SDK init failed: \(error)")
        }
    }
}

docs.title: Limelight iOS SDK
docs.text: Limelight is the main entry point of the Limelight iOS SDK. It provides SDK initialization, runtime configuration, and debug controls. All setup should happen at app launch, before any ads are requested.

docs.title: Initialization
docs.text:
Call Limelight.initialize(host:completion:) as early as possible — typically in application(_:didFinishLaunchingWithOptions:).

// AppDelegate.swift
import LimelightSDK

func application(_ application: UIApplication,
                 didFinishLaunchingWithOptions launchOptions: [UIApplication.LaunchOptionsKey: Any]?) -> Bool {

    Limelight.initialize(host: "https://ads.example.com") { result in
        switch result {
        case .success:
            print("Limelight SDK initialized")
        case .failure(let error):
            print("Initialization failed: \(error)")
        }
    }

    return true
}

The completion callback is optional. If you do not need to react to the initialization result, you can omit it:

Limelight.initialize(host: "https://ads.example.com")

docs.title: Runtime Configuration
docs.text:
Configure these static properties before or after initialization. Changes take effect on the next ad request.

Property Type Default Description adRequestTimeout Int (ms) 2000 HTTP timeout for ad requests loadAdTimeout TimeInterval (s) 6.0 Time allowed for a creative to load loadPrecachedAdTimeout TimeInterval (s) 30.0 Time allowed for video/interstitial creatives to load 
Limelight.adRequestTimeout = 3000
Limelight.loadAdTimeout = 8.0
Limelight.loadPrecachedAdTimeout = 45.0

docs.title: Custom Targeting
docs.text:
Attach custom targeting data to all ad requests via the targeting property.

let targeting = LimelightTargeting()
targeting.userAge = 25
targeting.keywords = ["sports", "tech"]

Limelight.targeting = targeting

docs.title: Log Level
docs.text:
Control the verbosity of SDK logs:

Limelight.logLevel = .verbose  // development
Limelight.logLevel = .error    // production

docs.title: Test Mode
docs.text:
Enable test mode to mark all bid requests as test traffic (sets bidRequest.test = 1):

Limelight.testMode = true  // never enable in production builds

docs.title: Privacy & Consent
docs.text:
Consent signals (TCF, CCPA, GPP) are read automatically from UserDefaults written by your CMP. The only signal configured via code is COPPA, set on Targeting.shared:

Targeting.shared.subjectToCOPPA = true

See the Consent Management guide for full details.

docs.title: SDK Version Info
docs.text:
print(Limelight.sdkVersion)    // e.g. "1.0.0"
print(Limelight.omsdkVersion)  // e.g. "1.4.2"

docs.title: Complete Setup Example
docs.text:
func application(_ application: UIApplication,
                 didFinishLaunchingWithOptions launchOptions: [UIApplication.LaunchOptionsKey: Any]?) -> Bool {

    // Debug (remove in production)
    Limelight.logLevel = .verbose
    Limelight.testMode = true

    // Runtime tuning
    Limelight.adRequestTimeout = 3000
    Limelight.loadPrecachedAdTimeout = 45.0

    // Targeting
    let targeting = LimelightTargeting()
    targeting.keywords = ["news", "finance"]
    Limelight.targeting = targeting

    // Initialize
    Limelight.initialize(host: "https://ads.example.com") { result in
        if case .failure(let error) = result {
            print("SDK init error: \(error)")
        }
    }

    return true
}

docs.title: LimelightInterstitialAd
docs.text: LimelightInterstitialAd loads and displays a full-screen interstitial ad. Load the ad in advance, wait for the delegate confirmation, then present it from a view controller.

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
Create an instance with an ad unit ID, set a delegate, and call loadAd().

import LimelightSDK

let interstitialAd = LimelightInterstitialAd(adUnitId: "your-ad-unit-id")
interstitialAd.delegate = self
interstitialAd.loadAd()

docs.title: Loading an Ad
docs.text:
Call loadAd() to begin fetching the ad. The SDK notifies you via onAdLoaded when the ad is ready to show.

interstitialAd.loadAd()

docs.title: Showing an Ad
docs.text:
Check isReady before presenting. Show the ad from a view controller once the ad is loaded.

func onAdLoaded(_ interstitialAd: LimelightInterstitialAd) {
    interstitialAd.show(from: self)
}

Note: show(from:) must be called on the main thread.

docs.title: Ad Sizes
docs.text:
By default the interstitial occupies the full screen. Use adSize to set the primary creative size and additionalCreativeSizes to allow the SDK to request additional sizes.

interstitialAd.adSize = CGSize(width: 320, height: 480)
interstitialAd.additionalCreativeSizes = [CGSize(width: 300, height: 250)]
interstitialAd.loadAd()

docs.title: Ad Formats
docs.text:
By default the SDK requests both .banner and .video formats. Set adFormats before calling loadAd() to restrict or change which formats are requested.

interstitialAd.adFormats = [.video]
interstitialAd.loadAd()

Supported formats: .banner, .video.

docs.title: SKAdNetwork
docs.text:
To enable an SKOverlay on supported devices, set supportSKOverlay before loading.

interstitialAd.supportSKOverlay = true
interstitialAd.loadAd()

docs.title: Delegate — Ad Lifecycle
docs.text:
Set delegate to an object conforming to InterstitialAdDelegate to receive ad lifecycle events. All callbacks are invoked on the main thread. All methods are optional.

Method When it fires onAdLoaded(_:) Ad loaded and ready to display onAdFailedToLoad(_:_:) Load error; inspect the Error for details onAdOpened(_:) Full-screen ad is being presented onAdImpression(_:) Ad became visible to the user onAdClicked(_:) User tapped the ad onAdClosed(_:) User dismissed the full-screen ad 
extension MyViewController: InterstitialAdDelegate {
    func onAdLoaded(_ interstitialAd: LimelightInterstitialAd) {
        interstitialAd.show(from: self)
    }

    func onAdFailedToLoad(_ interstitialAd: LimelightInterstitialAd, _ error: Error?) {
        print("Interstitial failed: \(String(describing: error))")
    }

    func onAdClosed(_ interstitialAd: LimelightInterstitialAd) {
        interstitialAd.loadAd() // preload the next ad
    }
}

docs.title: Initializer
docs.text:
init(adUnitId: String)

Creates an interstitial ad unit for the given ad unit ID.

docs.title: Properties
docs.text: Property Type Access Description delegate InterstitialAdDelegate? read/write Receives ad lifecycle callbacks isReady Bool read-only true when the ad is loaded and ready to display adSize CGSize read/write Primary creative size additionalCreativeSizes [CGSize]? read/write Extra sizes the ad slot can accept adFormats Set<AdFormat> read/write Creative formats to request. Defaults to [.banner, .video] adPosition AdPosition read/write Position of the ad on screen. Defaults to .fullScreen supportSKOverlay Bool read/write Enables SKOverlay on supported devices

docs.title: Methods
docs.text: Method Description loadAd() Starts loading the ad show(from:) Presents the ad from the given view controller; call only after isReady is true

docs.title: Complete Example
docs.text:
import UIKit
import LimelightSDK

class InterstitialViewController: UIViewController {

    private let interstitialAd = LimelightInterstitialAd(adUnitId: "your-ad-unit-id")

    override func viewDidLoad() {
        super.viewDidLoad()

        interstitialAd.delegate = self
        interstitialAd.loadAd()
    }
}

extension InterstitialViewController: InterstitialAdDelegate {
    func onAdLoaded(_ interstitialAd: LimelightInterstitialAd) {
        interstitialAd.show(from: self)
    }

    func onAdFailedToLoad(_ interstitialAd: LimelightInterstitialAd, _ error: Error?) {
        print("Interstitial ad failed: \(String(describing: error))")
    }

    func onAdClosed(_ interstitialAd: LimelightInterstitialAd) {
        interstitialAd.loadAd() // preload the next ad
    }
}

docs.title: LimelightRewardedAd
docs.text: LimelightRewardedAd loads and displays a full-screen rewarded ad. Load the ad in advance, wait for the delegate confirmation, present it from a view controller, then grant the reward when onAdRewarded fires.

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
Create an instance with an ad unit ID, set a delegate, and call loadAd().

import LimelightSDK

let rewardedAd = LimelightRewardedAd(adUnitId: "your-ad-unit-id")
rewardedAd.delegate = self
rewardedAd.loadAd()

docs.title: Loading an Ad
docs.text:
Call loadAd() to begin fetching the ad. The SDK notifies you via onAdLoaded when the ad is ready to show.

rewardedAd.loadAd()

docs.title: Showing an Ad
docs.text:
Check isReady before presenting. Show the ad from a view controller once the ad is loaded.

func onAdLoaded(_ rewardedAd: LimelightRewardedAd) {
    rewardedAd.show(from: self)
}

Note: show(from:) must be called on the main thread.

docs.title: Handling the Reward
docs.text:
Implement onAdRewarded to grant the in-app reward once the user has earned it. The LimelightReward object describes the reward type and amount defined for your ad unit.

func onAdRewarded(_ rewardedAd: LimelightRewardedAd, _ reward: LimelightReward) {
    // Grant the reward to the user
}

Important: Always grant the reward inside onAdRewarded, not onAdClosed. The reward callback fires only when the user has fulfilled the watch requirement; onAdClosed fires regardless.

docs.title: Ad Sizes
docs.text:
By default the rewarded ad occupies the full screen. Use adSize to set the primary creative size.

rewardedAd.adSize = CGSize(width: 320, height: 480)
rewardedAd.loadAd()

docs.title: Ad Formats
docs.text:
By default the SDK requests both .banner and .video formats. Set adFormats before calling loadAd() to restrict or change which formats are requested.

rewardedAd.adFormats = [.video]
rewardedAd.loadAd()

Supported formats: .banner, .video.

docs.title: SKAdNetwork
docs.text:
To enable an SKOverlay on supported devices, set supportSKOverlay before loading.

rewardedAd.supportSKOverlay = true
rewardedAd.loadAd()

docs.title: Delegate — Ad Lifecycle
docs.text:
Set delegate to an object conforming to RewardedAdDelegate to receive ad lifecycle events. All callbacks are invoked on the main thread. All methods are optional.

Method When it fires onAdLoaded(_:) Ad loaded and ready to display onAdFailedToLoad(_:_:) Load error; inspect the Error for details onAdOpened(_:) Full-screen ad is being presented onAdImpression(_:) Ad became visible to the user onAdClicked(_:) User tapped the ad onAdRewarded(_:_:) User earned the reward onAdClosed(_:) User dismissed the full-screen ad 
extension MyViewController: RewardedAdDelegate {
    func onAdLoaded(_ rewardedAd: LimelightRewardedAd) {
        rewardedAd.show(from: self)
    }

    func onAdFailedToLoad(_ rewardedAd: LimelightRewardedAd, _ error: Error?) {
        print("Rewarded ad failed: \(String(describing: error))")
    }

    func onAdRewarded(_ rewardedAd: LimelightRewardedAd, _ reward: LimelightReward) {
        // Grant the reward to the user
    }

    func onAdClosed(_ rewardedAd: LimelightRewardedAd) {
        rewardedAd.loadAd() // preload the next ad
    }
}

docs.title: Initializer
docs.text:
init(adUnitId: String)

Creates a rewarded ad unit for the given ad unit ID.

docs.title: Properties
docs.text: Property Type Access Description delegate RewardedAdDelegate? read/write Receives ad lifecycle callbacks isReady Bool read-only true when the ad is loaded and ready to display adSize CGSize read/write Primary creative size adFormats Set<AdFormat> read/write Creative formats to request. Defaults to [.banner, .video] adPosition AdPosition read/write Position of the ad on screen. Defaults to .fullScreen supportSKOverlay Bool read/write Enables SKOverlay on supported devices

docs.title: Methods
docs.text: Method Description loadAd() Starts loading the ad show(from:) Presents the ad from the given view controller; call only after isReady is true

docs.title: Complete Example
docs.text:
import UIKit
import LimelightSDK

class RewardedViewController: UIViewController {

    private let rewardedAd = LimelightRewardedAd(adUnitId: "your-ad-unit-id")

    override func viewDidLoad() {
        super.viewDidLoad()

        rewardedAd.delegate = self
        rewardedAd.loadAd()
    }
}

extension RewardedViewController: RewardedAdDelegate {
    func onAdLoaded(_ rewardedAd: LimelightRewardedAd) {
        rewardedAd.show(from: self)
    }

    func onAdFailedToLoad(_ rewardedAd: LimelightRewardedAd, _ error: Error?) {
        print("Rewarded ad failed: \(String(describing: error))")
    }

    func onAdRewarded(_ rewardedAd: LimelightRewardedAd, _ reward: LimelightReward) {
        // Grant the reward to the user
    }

    func onAdClosed(_ rewardedAd: LimelightRewardedAd) {
        rewardedAd.loadAd() // preload the next ad
    }
}
